name: BorrowBoard
tagline: Turn borrowed items into a game.
description: BorrowBoard is a social board game app that helps neighbors, roommates, clubs, and small communities keep track of shared items without awkward reminders or lost stuff. Players build a digital game board around real-world objects like power tools, books, cooking gear, sports equipment, or event supplies, and every loan becomes a move with a due date, value, and accountability score. The app solves the everyday problem of borrowing friction by making tracking feel playful instead of bureaucratic, which reduces forgotten returns, duplicate purchases, and uncomfortable follow-ups. In daily life, a parent can lend a snow shovel to a neighbor, a coworker can borrow a portable projector for a presentation, or a friend group can rotate camping gear while the app reminds everyone who has what and when it should come back. What makes it unique is that the board changes dynamically based on trust, timeliness, and item importance, so good habits unlock bonuses while repeated late returns create new challenges and visibility. It also supports in-person play modes for households or community groups, turning shared ownership into a cooperative experience rather than a messy spreadsheet. Unlike generic lending trackers, BorrowBoard uses game mechanics to make real-life accountability feel rewarding, social, and easy to stick with.
features: The Loan Trail board maps every borrowed item as a game token, showing who has it, when it is due, and how long it has been out so users can see their shared inventory at a glance.
A trust-and-timing scoring system rewards on-time returns with bonuses and unlocks cooperative perks, because positive reinforcement makes recurring borrowing behavior easier to manage.
Smart reminder quests send playful nudges through the app at the right moment, helping users follow up without sounding rude or forgetting to check in.

Household and community boards let groups like roommates, PTA members, or maker clubs create private spaces for shared items, which matters because shared ownership often spans multiple people and schedules.
A swap-and-lend history view records patterns across items and people, helping users spot frequent borrow cycles and decide when to buy, share, or set clearer rules.

audience: "Adults aged 20-45 who live with roommates, participate in neighborhood groups, or frequently share equipment in clubs, offices, or families. It is especially useful for busy people who borrow and lend items often but do not want to manage them with spreadsheets or awkward text reminders."
